duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Router._validateDecimals uint8 unsigned subtraction underflows when baseDecimals > quoteDecimals, blocking valid pool creation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six describe the identical uint8 underflow in _validateDecimals(uint8,uint8) in Router.sol lines 598-603. Same code defect, same impact (DoS for pool creation), same preconditions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MagicLP.flashLoan charges zero fees when borrower returns both tokens such that both balances >= reserves
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eight describe the same control-flow gap in flashLoan where neither fee-charging branch executes if both post-callback balances >= reserves. Same code path (MagicLP.sol lines ~247-314), same exploit (free flash loan), same impact (lost fee revenue). Note: TECH2-H003 refers to the entry titled 'Fee Evasion in flashLoan via Equal or Greater Balance Returns'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MagicLP sellBase/sellQuote/flashLoan use tx.origin instead of msg.sender for fee rate determination, enabling fee manipulation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six describe the tx.origin usage in querySellBase/querySellQuote for fee rate lookup. Same code pattern (MagicLP.sol lines 208-209, 228-229), same exploit mechanism (intermediary contracts inherit EOA fee rates), same config dependency. Note: TECH2-H007 refers to the entry titled 'Fee Rate Model Bypass via tx.origin in Swap Functions'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Maintainer fee (mtFee) extraction during swaps/flash loans causes systematic target-reserve drift eroding LP value
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same accounting issue: mtFee is transferred out of pool balance before _sync/_setReserve without adjusting targets, causing reserves to systematically drift below targets. Same root cause in sellBase/sellQuote/flashLoan code paths.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permissionless correctRState() creates arbitrage opportunities by resetting PMM curve discontinuously after accumulated target-reserve drift
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five describe exploiting the permissionless correctRState() to extract value from LPs after target-reserve drift. OPS-H015 adds flash-loan amplification and ECON2-H016 adds sync() as a contributing factor, but the exploited vulnerability is the same: correctRState resets pricing discontinuously and is permissionless. OPS-H002 has the most complete description of the attack chain.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Non-transferable reward token permanently bricks all reward claims in LockingMultiRewards due to lack of try-catch and no removeReward function
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five describe the same vulnerability: _getRewards iterates all rewardTokens with safeTransfer and no try-catch; one non-transferable token blocks all claims; no removeReward exists. ECON-H005 provides the most comprehensive impact analysis including total value at risk across all reward tokens.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: sellShares uses divCeil for target reduction but floor division for payouts, causing cumulative asymmetric rounding that erodes targets
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five describe the identical asymmetric rounding in sellShares (divCeil for _BASE_TARGET_/_QUOTE_TARGET_ reduction vs floor division for baseAmount/quoteAmount). Same code location (MagicLP.sol sellShares), same cumulative drift mechanism. Note: TECH2-H004 refers to the entry titled 'Target-Reserve Desync via Asymmetric Rounding in sellShares'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: processExpiredLocks cannot process the lock at lastLockIndex when other locks exist, creating forced ordering delays
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same code constraint: `if (index == lastLockIndex[user] && locks.length > 1) revert ErrInvalidLockIndex()` prevents processing the most recently created lock when other locks remain. Same code location, same impact (delayed fund unlocking).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permissionless sync() allows attackers to incorporate donated/excess tokens into reserves without target adjustment, enabling pricing manipulation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four describe abusing permissionless sync() to update reserves from balanceOf without target/RState adjustment. TECH-H013 and OPS2-H012 focus on donation attacks; OPS-H013 adds rebasing/fee-on-transfer token angle. Same root cause: sync() updates reserves but not targets. Note: TECH2-H003 here refers to the entry titled 'Permissionless sync() enables donation-driven reserve skew'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee-on-transfer reward tokens cause LockingMultiRewards insolvency because notifyRewardAmount sets rewardRate based on nominal amount, not actual received
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four describe the same bug: notifyRewardAmount uses `amount` (not balance delta) to compute rewardRate after safeTransferFrom. If token charges transfer fee, contract is insolvent. Same code location (notifyRewardAmount), same impact (reward claim reverts).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Router multi-hop swap functions accept arbitrary path addresses without validating they are genuine Factory-registered MagicLP pools
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same missing validation: Router transfers tokens to path[0] and calls sellBase/sellQuote on each path element without checking Factory registry or code hash. Same exploit (malicious contract steals intermediate tokens), same code location (_swap/_validatePath in Router.sol).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MagicLP.ratioSync() reverts with division-by-zero when a reserve is 0, removing an admin recovery path
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same division-by-zero in ratioSync() when _BASE_RESERVE_ or _QUOTE_RESERVE_ is 0. Same code location (ratioSync), same impact (admin recovery bricked).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Rewards are permanently lost when notifyRewardAmount is called near epoch boundary and totalSupply is zero during the distribution window
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same issue: when rewardRate is high (near epoch end) and totalSupply is 0, rewardPerToken does not increase, permanently stranding rewards. ECON2-H005 generalizes to any zero-supply period. Same root cause (_rewardPerToken returns stored value when totalSupply=0 while lastUpdateTime advances).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Factory.create uses tx.origin for creator attribution and CREATE2 salt, breaking smart contract wallet composability and causing salt collisions
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same tx.origin usage in Factory.create for salt computation and userPools registration. Same code (Factory.sol line ~61 `address creator = tx.origin`), same impacts (misattribution, salt collision). Note: TECH2-H005 here refers to the entry titled 'Smart Contract Composability Broken by tx.origin in Factory Salt Computation'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Integer division truncation in notifyRewardAmount permanently strands dust reward tokens in the contract
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same precision loss: `rewardRate = amount / _remainingRewardTime` truncates, leaving unrecoverable dust. Same code location and impact. Note: TECH2-H006 here refers to the entry titled 'Permanent Stranding of Reward Tokens due to Reward Rate Truncation'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LockingMultiRewards withdraw/getRewards lack reentrancy guard; callback-capable tokens (ERC777-style) could reenter and corrupt accounting
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same missing reentrancy protection in LockingMultiRewards withdraw() and _getRewards() combined with external token transfers that could enable reentrant callbacks. Same code paths, same config dependency (callback-capable token).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Pool permanently bricked when any reserve reaches zero (via admin setParameters, extreme rounding, or other path) — buyShares, swaps, getMidPrice, and Router liquidity all revert
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six describe consequences of the same zero-reserve state: TECH2-H001/ECON2-H006/OPS2-H006 focus on buyShares falling through branches; ECON2-H013 focuses on getMidPrice division-by-zero via _twapUpdate; ECON2-H015 focuses on Router._adjustAddLiquidity returning (0,0). OPS-H007 is rated CRITICAL and covers both the trigger (admin setParameters) and cascading consequences across buyShares, sellBase, sellQuote, and Math functions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PMM math functions (_GeneralIntegrate, _SolveQuadraticFunctionForTarget, DecimalMath) overflow on large reserves or extreme pool parameters, causing DoS
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe integer overflow in PMM math calculations with large parameters. ECON2-H014 covers _GeneralIntegrate overflow with MAX_I=10^36. ECON2-H017 covers _SolveQuadraticFunctionForTarget ki*delta overflow. TECH2-H007 (entry titled 'Math._GeneralIntegrate and DecimalMath mul/div can overflow') covers both _GeneralIntegrate and DecimalMath. Same root cause (unchecked multiplication in Math.sol/DecimalMath.sol), same impact (swap/query DoS). ECON2-H014 has the strongest evidence and highest severity.
